untranslatable_reason: Signal relies on order book microstructure analysis (hidden order detection via IOC ping probes, bid-ask spread dynamics, execution priority sequencing, and order timing logic) that cannot be expressed using only OHLCV-derived technical indicators. Requires: (1) real-time order book depth data, (2) order execution latency measurements, (3) hidden volume inference from order fills, (4) time-between-orders state tracking across multiple probe attempts. The core mechanism is algorithmic order flow manipulation, not price/volume pattern recognition.
translation_notes: This is an institutional market-making/liquidity detection strategy that operates at the order execution layer rather than the price chart layer. It cannot be backtested using standard OHLCV data. Would require: tick-by-tick order book snapshots, latency measurements, and execution simulation engine capable of modeling hidden liquidity pools and order priority rules.
